toolchain: nvcc /usr/local/cuda-12.9/bin/nvcc (Tue May 27 2025 build) -arch=compute_90 + driver JIT to sm_120

kernel_a_trans_behavior.interpretation: matches NEITHER (both > 1e-2 tolerance) -- the .trans + .row.col combination at a single 16x16 mma fragment produces a partial-block mapping (round-7 finding: 8x8-block transpose only, not full 16x16 transpose), so the kernel computes a non-trivial intermediate that does NOT equal a clean mat-mul against EITHER B or B^T

kernel_b_pretrans_behavior.compute: V pre-transpose in smem (sVt[n][k] = V[k][n]) + ldmatrix.x4 non-trans + mma.row.col
kernel_b_pretrans_behavior.interpretation: MATCH (err << 1e-3) -- the V pre-transpose work-around STILL holds at BM=32 BK=32 tile shape, exactly as round-7 probe3 reported at the round-7 64x64-tile shape

trans_behavior_at_BK32: 8x8-block (same as round-7 finding at the larger 64x64 tile)
honest_scope: Single-shape probe at BM=32 BK=32 confirms the round-7 mma-fragment-map property is invariant to the surrounding tile shape (as expected: the mma is atomic at m16n8k16, fragment-map is a property of the mma instruction itself, not the surrounding tile). Doesn't generalize to OTHER (BM, BK) wedge variants without re-probing, though the same atomic-mma reasoning suggests they would all match. Probe does NOT directly verify there is no smem-bank-conflict in the pre-transpose smem layout under high-contention multi-warp access -- that's a separate (lighter) measurement at full-kernel build time.
implication_for_bc4_round14: Risk-d CLEARED for cheap-first oracle gate. BC4 round-14 silicon fire (flash_attn_bm32_occupancy_v0) should proceed with V pre-transpose budgeted in smem per plan section 2 (BK*d*2 = 32*64*2 = 4096 B V^T slot). Plan section 2 smem budget table (BM=32 BK=32 O-reg = 30720 B) is correct, no replan needed. The BM=32 BK=32 wedge keeps its 3 CTAs/SM occupancy projection.
